text:
are animals used for navigation and travel across vast terrain, usually in Titan-infested territory outside the Walls. They are used on expeditions led by the Scout Regiment for hauling supplies and provisions, and they are also used for leading carriages, hauling carts, and general travel of merchants and civilians within the Walls. Like all other non-human animals, their presence is completely ignored by Titans.

It is virtually impossible to escape the grasp of a Titan without a horse outside the Walls, excluding any Forests of Giant Trees where omni-directional mobility gear can be utilized. Horses stand around 160 cm, weighing between 450 and 500 kg. They can also maintain a speed of 35 kilometers an hour, with their top speed ranging from 75 to 80 kilometers an hour.

Breeding

After the fall of Wall Maria, humanity lost a considerable amount of its territory and resources. In an attempt to make ends meet, the government reached out to rural hunting villages like Dauper in hopes of recruiting them as hired horse breeders.

Biologically, horses were specially bred to allow the Scout Regiment to outrun Titans during expeditions outside the Walls. They are not frightened by Titans and are also capable of running long distances without exhaustion. For instance, when Tomas was sent by Miche Zacharius to alert the military following a breach in Wall Rose, he had traveled for 12 hours by horse before arriving at Stohess District.

Notable Traits

Speed - They have a top speed of , making them ideal additions to the Scout Regiment.

Endurance - They can maintain a constant speed of . Due to their staunch legs, they are capable of withstanding the stress of sudden starts, acceleration, and circling. They are also capable of reaching a speed of while pulling a wagon.

Temperament - They are quite docile and are not normally frightened in the presence of Titans. The nearly constant sound of omni-directional mobility gear being used so close to the horses also seems to have no effect on them.

Trivia

Horses are noted to be extremely expensive, costing about as much as an average citizen's lifetime earnings. As such, the Scout Regiment are usually the only ones to have them.
They are considered, like human soldiers, a part of the Scout Regiment. If they die, it is suggested that you bring back as much of its mane as possible for a burial.
The real-life equivalent of the horses seen in the series is the Thoroughbred.
